Creating Prelaunch Video:
Part Three
Module 4
Creating Full Motion video
• You need a good script
• Effectiveness varies with “talent”… but you don’t
have to be an actor
• People want YOU, not an actor
• You will get better with practice…
• It’s much easier with a teleprompter
Creating Full Motion video
• Teleprompters don’t work outside
• Complicated… just plain trickier all around
• Some people use “green screen”
• Harder to edit
• Can also be “quick and dirty”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	2
Creating Full Motion video - Cameras
• Lots of options, and they’re always changing
• It’s not about the camera
• I’ve made super successful videos with a $100
camera
• You can even use your phone
• External audio input is very important
• Audio monitoring is VERY nice to have
Creating Full Motion video - Cameras
• Your phone is one option - especially if you can
use external audio
• A video camera is an option - constantly
changing, we’ve used the Canon Vixia in the past
• Another option - DSLR - we’re currently using
Canon 5d and Canon 6d (expensive)
• Remember - it’s not about the camera
Creating Full Motion video – Audio is super
important
• Watch for wind noise when outside
• Use an external mic
• Lavalier mics (or lavalier mics or lapel mics) come
in wired and wireless… starting at $30
• We use the Sennheiser EW112PG2 (very
expensive)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Creating Full Motion video –
Teleprompters
• Very nice to have, if only for bullet points
• You look directly into the camera while reading
from the teleprompter
• Can be expensive, but you can make your own
with an iPad
• I got mine at PrompterPeople.com (expensive)
Use a Tripod
Creating Full Motion video - Editing
software
• Mac
• iMovie
• Adobe Premiere
• Final Cut
Creating Full Motion video - Editing
software
• Windows
• Adobe Premiere
• Sony Vegas
• Many others
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
